Description: It provides a suite of command-line tools to run BLAST. This allows users to perform BLAST searches on their own server without size, volume and database restrictions.
It is a versatile toolkit designed for assembly and analysis of sequencing data. SPAdes is primarily developed for Illumina sequencing data, but can be used for IonTorrent as well.
It is an ultra-fast and memory-efficient NGS assembler. It is optimized for metagenomes, but also works well on generic single genome assembly and single-cell assembly.

It is an easy-to-use metagenomic wrapper suite that accomplishes the core tasks of metagenomic analysis from start to finish: read quality control, assembly, visualization, taxonomic profiling, extracting draft genomes, and functional annotation.
Effective binning of metagenomic contigs using contrastive multi-view representation learning
It is a command tool for metagenomic binning with deep learning, handles both short and long reads.
It is a robust statistical framework for reconstructing genomes from metagenomic data
It is an automated method that integrates the results of a flexible number of binning algorithms to calculate an optimized, non-redundant set of bins from a single assembly
It compares and "de-replicate" a genome set by identifying groups of highly similar genomes and choosing the best representative genome for each genome set.
It is a program for clustering biological sequences to reduce sequence redundancy and improve the performance of other sequence analyses.
Detect CRISPR-Cas genes and arrays, and predict the subtype based on both Cas genes and CRISPR repeat sequence.
It is a software tool for identifying antiviral defence systems in prokaryotic genomes.
It is a software tool for identifying antiviral defence systems and anti-defence in prokaryotic genomes and viral genomes.
Estimate average coverage and create Nonpareil curves for metagenomic datasets. Aldso calculates a diversity index.
It enables the prediction of metabolic and biogeochemical functional trait profiles to any given genome datasets. These genome datasets can either be MAGs, SAGs or isolated strains.
it is a tool for annotating metagenomic and genomic assembled data (e.g. scaffolds or contigs) or called genes (e.g. nuclotide or amino acid format)

It is a database which integrates together predictive information about proteins' function from a number of partner resources, giving an overview of the families that a protein belongs to and the domains and sites it contains.
It is a software tool to annotate bacterial, archaeal and viral genomes quickly and produce standards-compliant output files.
It is a tool that uses two distinct machine learning models to predict genome completeness.

It is a fully automated command-line pipeline for assessing the quality of single-contig viral genomes, including identification of host contamination for integrated proviruses, estimating completeness for genome fragments, and identification of closed genomes.
It is a toll that Identify viruses and plasmids in sequencing data (isolates, metagenomes, and metatranscriptomes)
It is an automated command-line pipeline for predicting host genus of novel bacteriophages and archaeoviruses based on their genome sequences.
It is an integrated pipeline for large-scale phylogenetic profiling of genomes and metagenomes.
It is a tool that aims to be a configurable, easy to use and fast DNA read coverage and relative abundance calculator focused on metagenomics applications.
It is a wrapper around Cutadapt and FastQC to consistently apply adapter and quality trimming to FastQ files, with extra functionality for RRBS data.
It is a program for the taxonomic classification of high-throughput sequencing reads, e.g., Illumina or Roche/454, from whole-genome sequencing of metagenomic DNA
It is a software toolkit for assigning objective taxonomic classifications to bacterial and archaeal genomes based on the Genome Database Taxonomy (GTDB).
Assigns taxonomy to putative giant virus contigs or metagenome assembled genomes
It is a taxonomic sequence classification system
It is an alignment-based tool for fast and accurate calculation of Average Nucleotide Identity (ANI) between complete or metagenomically-assembled viral genomes
It is a computational tool that makes interpretable predictions of a binary outcome from joint metabolomics and microbial sequencing (16S rRNA amplicon analysis or metagenomics) profiles
It is a computational tool for species-level microbial profiling (bacteria, archaea, eukaryotes, and viruses) from metagenomic shotgun sequencing data.
This tool streamlines and standardizes the taxonomic categorization of prokaryotic viral genomes

It is a program designed to spot potential problems in high througput sequencing datasets. It runs a set of analyses on one or more raw sequence files in fastq or bam format and produces a report which summarises the results.

It evaluates genome/metagenome assemblies by computing various metrics. The current QUAST toolkit includes the general QUAST tool for genome assemblies, MetaQUAST, the extension for metagenomic datasets, QUAST-LG, the extension for large genomes.
It is a python-based tool for genome-scale metabolic model reconstruction.
It is a genomes-to-report pipeline. It accepts prokaryotic (bacterial and archaeal) genomic assemblies and compares them in many different ways.
It is a rapid standardised annotation tool for bacteriophage genomes and metagenomes.
It is a sensitive annotation tool for bacteriophage genomes and metagenomes using protein structural homology.
It is a tool for the rapid & standardized annotation of bacterial genomes and plasmids from both isolates and MAGs
